Introduction.

This is a worksheet for use with Lecture 11.

You have a videos of me narrating these slides. Note that there are potentially minor discrepancies between the current set of slides and the one in the video. The slide numbers refer to the current set. I do not cover every single slide but you can code along!

If you answer correctly the colour of the box will change from red dashed to full blue! (Don’t worry about bonus questions, they are very much just that: a bonus!)

Slides

Look at the pretty, pretty. (Slide 5)

True or False.

The lines represent LOESS fits.

Try it yourself (Slide 14).

Use the same code from the previous slides.

Pick any 3 schools.

Rcmdr (Slide 26)

Bonus

Try and install ‘Rcmdr’. if you feel this is a risk then don’t do it

If you run into issues check these notes

Going further.

There is no exercise associated with this session but I urge you to explore the other fabulous things which R can do.

  • Have a look at resources made by Alex and Sarah.
  • Have a look at the resources listed at the end.

PS. Do you want to have the equations for your (multilevel) model, then check this package

Session Info.

Thanks to Lisa DeBruine for the webex package. Please see general disclaimer.
